source: Kenya RDM 6.3 (2025) Chapter 2 -- Traffic Signals and Communication System
note: Warrant criteria extracted from RDM 6.3 Tables 2.1, 2.2, and Section 2.2. PCU values from RDM 6.3 Table 2.3 (Traffic Signal Design column).

warrants.description: Minimum vehicle volumes -- 8-hour criterion. Signal justified when traffic volume in pcu exceeds thresholds for 8 or more hours on an average day.

Applied when traffic volume on the major road is so heavy that minor road traffic suffers excessive delay or hazard. Satisfied when pcu volumes exceed thresholds for any 8-hour period on an average day.

Applied when pedestrians experience excessive delay in crossing. Applicable if 600+ pcu/hr enter the intersection (both approaches) on the major road AND 150+ pedestrians cross the major road per hour.

thresholds.median_condition: Also applicable if raised median >= 1.5 m width AND 1000+ pcu/hr on major road

warrants.reduced_thresholds_note: 70% of requirement applies where 85th percentile speed > 60 km/h or in built-up area of population < 150,000

warrants.description: Applied when severity and frequency of crashes are principal reasons. Signal can be installed if alternatives were tried but were not effective.

thresholds.crash_type: each with injury and property damage
thresholds.condition: which could have been prevented by installing a traffic signal

warrants.description: If no single factor provides the warrant, two or more warrants may be considered together if they meet 80% or more of the stated criteria.
warrants.note: Adequate trial of other remedial measures should be tried before installing a signal.

pcu_values.table_reference: RDM 6.3 Table 2.3 -- Traffic Signal Design column
